FML - Clustering - April1st

Aamir Sohaib

2026-04-02

Loading dataset into R environment

pharma <- read.csv("/Users/roc/Downloads/Pharmaceuticals-1.csv")

Understanding basics of finacial data

str(pharma)
## 'data.frame':    21 obs. of  14 variables:
##  $ Symbol               : chr  "ABT" "AGN" "AHM" "AZN" ...
##  $ Name                 : chr  "Abbott Laboratories" "Allergan, Inc." "Amersham plc" "AstraZeneca PLC" ...
##  $ Market_Cap           : num  68.44 7.58 6.3 67.63 47.16 ...
##  $ Beta                 : num  0.32 0.41 0.46 0.52 0.32 1.11 0.5 0.85 1.08 0.18 ...
##  $ PE_Ratio             : num  24.7 82.5 20.7 21.5 20.1 27.9 13.9 26 3.6 27.9 ...
##  $ ROE                  : num  26.4 12.9 14.9 27.4 21.8 3.9 34.8 24.1 15.1 31 ...
##  $ ROA                  : num  11.8 5.5 7.8 15.4 7.5 1.4 15.1 4.3 5.1 13.5 ...
##  $ Asset_Turnover       : num  0.7 0.9 0.9 0.9 0.6 0.6 0.9 0.6 0.3 0.6 ...
##  $ Leverage             : num  0.42 0.6 0.27 0 0.34 0 0.57 3.51 1.07 0.53 ...
##  $ Rev_Growth           : num  7.54 9.16 7.05 15 26.81 ...
##  $ Net_Profit_Margin    : num  16.1 5.5 11.2 18 12.9 2.6 20.6 7.5 13.3 23.4 ...
##  $ Median_Recommendation: chr  "Moderate Buy" "Moderate Buy" "Strong Buy" "Moderate Sell" ...
##  $ Location             : chr  "US" "CANADA" "UK" "UK" ...
##  $ Exchange             : chr  "NYSE" "NYSE" "NYSE" "NYSE" ...
summary(pharma)
##     Symbol              Name             Market_Cap          Beta       
##  Length:21          Length:21          Min.   :  0.41   Min.   :0.1800  
##  Class :character   Class :character   1st Qu.:  6.30   1st Qu.:0.3500

PART (a):Cluster using only numerical variables

df_num <- pharma[, 3:11]
rownames(df_num) <- pharma$Name
print(df_num)
##                                    Market_Cap Beta PE_Ratio  ROE  ROA
## Abbott Laboratories                     68.44 0.32     24.7 26.4 11.8

Z-Score Normalization

Now I scale the data using z-score normalization to remove scale bias. Without scaling, variables with larger ranges would dominate the distance calculations unfairly.

df_scaled <- scale(df_num)
print(df_scaled)
##                                    Market_Cap        Beta    PE_Ratio
## Abbott Laboratories                 0.1840960 -0.80125356 -0.04671323
## Allergan, Inc.                     -0.8544181 -0.45070513  3.49706911

##        57.6514286         0.5257143        25.4619048        25.7952381

##        15.6952381 
## attr(,"scaled:scale")
##        Market_Cap              Beta          PE_Ratio               ROE 
##        58.6029595         0.2567406        16.3102568        15.0849752 
##               ROA    Asset_Turnover          Leverage        Rev_Growth 
##         5.3213988         0.2167948         0.7813103        11.0483351 
## Net_Profit_Margin 
##         6.5620482

Now I compute Euclidean distances between all pairs of firms

distance <- get_dist(df_scaled)
fviz_dist(distance,gradient= list(low="#00AFBB",mid = "white", high= "#FC4E07"),
          lab_size = 6) +labs(title ="Distance Matrix: Pharmaceutical Firms")

Now I determine Optimal Number of Clusters (k)

Method-1: Elbow Method

set.seed(123)
fviz_nbclust(df_scaled,kmeans, method = "wss") +labs(title= "Elbow Method: Optimal Number of Clusters",x ="Number of Clusters (k)",y = "Total Within-Cluster Sum of Squares (WSS)")

Method 2: Silhouette Method

set.seed(123)
fviz_nbclust(df_scaled,kmeans, method = "silhouette") +labs(title ="Silhouette Method: Optimal Number of Clusters",x ="Number of Clusters (k)",y ="Average Silhouette Width")

Now I run K-Means clustering with k=5. Based on Elbow and Silhouette methods, k = 5 is selected as optimal.

set.seed(123)
k5 <- kmeans(df_scaled,centers =5,nstart= 25)
print(k5$centers)
##    Market_Cap       Beta    PE_Ratio        ROE        ROA Asset_Turnover
## 1 -0.03142211 -0.4360989 -0.31724852  0.1950459  0.4083915      0.1729746
## 2 -0.87051511  1.3409869 -0.05284434 -0.6184015 -1.1928478     -0.4612656
## 3 -0.43925134 -0.4701800  2.70002464 -0.8349525 -0.9234951      0.2306328
## 4  1.69558112 -0.1780563 -0.19845823  1.2349879  1.3503431      1.1531640
## 5 -0.76022489  0.2796041 -0.47742380 -0.7438022 -0.8107428     -1.2684804
##      Leverage Rev_Growth Net_Profit_Margin
## 1 -0.27449312 -0.7041516       0.556954446
## 2  1.36644699 -0.6912914      -1.320000179
## 3 -0.14170336 -0.1168459      -1.416514761
## 4 -0.46807818  0.4671788       0.591242521
## 5  0.06308085  1.5180158      -0.006893899

to view size of each cluster

print(k5$size)
## [1] 8 3 2 4 4

to view cluster assignment for each firm

print(k5$cluster)
##                Abbott Laboratories                     Allergan, Inc. 
##                                  1                                  3 
##                       Amersham plc                    AstraZeneca PLC 
##                                  1                                  1 
##                            Aventis                           Bayer AG 
##                                  5                                  2 
##       Bristol-Myers Squibb Company                       Chattem, Inc 
##                                  1                                  2 
##              Elan Corporation, plc              Eli Lilly and Company 
##                                  5                                  1 
##                GlaxoSmithKline plc                   IVAX Corporation 
##                                  4                                  2 
##                  Johnson & Johnson Medicis Pharmaceutical Corporation 
##                                  4                                  5 
##                  Merck & Co., Inc.                        Novartis AG 
##                                  4                                  1 
##                         Pfizer Inc              Pharmacia Corporation 
##                                  4                                  3 
##        Schering-Plough Corporation       Watson Pharmaceuticals, Inc. 
##                                  1                                  5 
##                              Wyeth 
##                                  1

Now I visualize the clusters

fviz_cluster(k5, data=df_scaled,palette = c("#2E9FDF","#E7B800","#FC4E07","#00BA38","#9b59b6"), ellipse.type= "euclid",repel=TRUE,ggtheme = theme_minimal()) +
  labs(title ="K-Means Clustering (k=5): Pharmaceutical Firms")
## Too few points to calculate an ellipse
## Too few points to calculate an ellipse

PART (b): Interpretetion of clusters based on numerical variables

pharma$Cluster <- k5$cluster
cluster_summary <- pharma %>%
  group_by(Cluster) %>%
  summarise(
    Firms        = n(),
    Avg_MarketCap =mean(Market_Cap),
    Avg_Beta      =mean(Beta),
    Avg_PE_Ratio  =mean(PE_Ratio),
    Avg_ROE       =mean(ROE),
    Avg_ROA       =mean(ROA),
    Avg_AssetTO   =mean(Asset_Turnover),
    Avg_Leverage  =mean(Leverage),
    Avg_RevGrowth =mean(Rev_Growth),
    Avg_NetMargin =mean(Net_Profit_Margin))
print(cluster_summary)
## # A tibble: 5 × 11
##   Cluster Firms Avg_MarketCap Avg_Beta Avg_PE_Ratio Avg_ROE Avg_ROA Avg_AssetTO
##     <int> <int>         <dbl>    <dbl>        <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>       <dbl>
## 1       1     8         55.8     0.414         20.3    28.7   12.7        0.738
## 2       2     3          6.64    0.87          24.6    16.5    4.17       0.6  
## 3       3     2         31.9     0.405         69.5    13.2    5.6        0.75 
## 4       4     4        157.      0.48          22.2    44.4   17.7        0.95 
## 5       5     4         13.1     0.598         17.7    14.6    6.2        0.425
## # ℹ 3 more variables: Avg_Leverage <dbl>, Avg_RevGrowth <dbl>,
## #   Avg_NetMargin <dbl>
pharma %>%
  select(Name, Cluster) %>%
  arrange(Cluster) %>%
  print()
##                                  Name Cluster
## 1                 Abbott Laboratories       1
## 2                        Amersham plc       1
## 3                     AstraZeneca PLC       1
## 4        Bristol-Myers Squibb Company       1
## 5               Eli Lilly and Company       1
## 6                         Novartis AG       1
## 7         Schering-Plough Corporation       1
## 8                               Wyeth       1
## 9                            Bayer AG       2
## 10                       Chattem, Inc       2
## 11                   IVAX Corporation       2
## 12                     Allergan, Inc.       3
## 13              Pharmacia Corporation       3
## 14                GlaxoSmithKline plc       4
## 15                  Johnson & Johnson       4
## 16                  Merck & Co., Inc.       4
## 17                         Pfizer Inc       4
## 18                            Aventis       5
## 19              Elan Corporation, plc       5
## 20 Medicis Pharmaceutical Corporation       5
## 21       Watson Pharmaceuticals, Inc.       5

PART (c): Pattern in non-numerical variables(10-12)

cat("\n Median Recommendation by Cluster \n")
## 
##  Median Recommendation by Cluster
pharma %>%
  select(Name, Cluster, Median_Recommendation) %>%
  arrange(Cluster) %>%
  print()
##                                  Name Cluster Median_Recommendation
## 1                 Abbott Laboratories       1          Moderate Buy
## 2                        Amersham plc       1            Strong Buy
## 3                     AstraZeneca PLC       1         Moderate Sell
## 4        Bristol-Myers Squibb Company       1         Moderate Sell
## 5               Eli Lilly and Company       1                  Hold
## 6                         Novartis AG       1                  Hold
## 7         Schering-Plough Corporation       1                  Hold
## 8                               Wyeth       1                  Hold
## 9                            Bayer AG       2                  Hold
## 10                       Chattem, Inc       2          Moderate Buy
## 11                   IVAX Corporation       2                  Hold
## 12                     Allergan, Inc.       3          Moderate Buy
## 13              Pharmacia Corporation       3                  Hold
## 14                GlaxoSmithKline plc       4                  Hold
## 15                  Johnson & Johnson       4          Moderate Buy
## 16                  Merck & Co., Inc.       4                  Hold
## 17                         Pfizer Inc       4          Moderate Buy
## 18                            Aventis       5          Moderate Buy
## 19              Elan Corporation, plc       5         Moderate Sell
## 20 Medicis Pharmaceutical Corporation       5          Moderate Buy
## 21       Watson Pharmaceuticals, Inc.       5         Moderate Sell

Now I count each recommendation type per cluster

rec_pattern <- pharma %>%
  group_by(Cluster, Median_Recommendation) %>%
  summarise(Count = n(), .groups = "drop")
print(rec_pattern)
## # A tibble: 12 × 3
##    Cluster Median_Recommendation Count
##      <int> <chr>                 <int>
##  1       1 Hold                      4
##  2       1 Moderate Buy              1
##  3       1 Moderate Sell             2
##  4       1 Strong Buy                1
##  5       2 Hold                      2
##  6       2 Moderate Buy              1
##  7       3 Hold                      1
##  8       3 Moderate Buy              1
##  9       4 Hold                      2
## 10       4 Moderate Buy              2
## 11       5 Moderate Buy              2
## 12       5 Moderate Sell             2

Pattern: Location (HQ Country) per Cluster

cat("\n Location (HQ Country) by Cluster \n")
## 
##  Location (HQ Country) by Cluster
loc_pattern <- pharma %>%
  group_by(Cluster, Location) %>%
  summarise(Count = n(), .groups = "drop")
print(loc_pattern)
## # A tibble: 12 × 3
##    Cluster Location    Count
##      <int> <chr>       <int>
##  1       1 SWITZERLAND     1
##  2       1 UK              2
##  3       1 US              5
##  4       2 GERMANY         1
##  5       2 US              2
##  6       3 CANADA          1
##  7       3 US              1
##  8       4 UK              1
##  9       4 US              3
## 10       5 FRANCE          1
## 11       5 IRELAND         1
## 12       5 US              2

Pattern: Stock Exchange per Cluster

cat("\n Stock Exchange by Cluster \n")
## 
##  Stock Exchange by Cluster
exc_pattern <- pharma %>%
  group_by(Cluster, Exchange) %>%
  summarise(Count = n(), .groups = "drop")
print(exc_pattern)
## # A tibble: 7 × 3
##   Cluster Exchange Count
##     <int> <chr>    <int>
## 1       1 NYSE         8
## 2       2 AMEX         1
## 3       2 NASDAQ       1
## 4       2 NYSE         1
## 5       3 NYSE         2
## 6       4 NYSE         4
## 7       5 NYSE         4

PART (d): Name Each Cluster

Based on cluster_summary above, I assign descriptive names.These names reflect dominant financial characteristics of each group.

cluster_names <- data.frame(
  Cluster = 1:5,
  Cluster_Name = c(
    "Large-Cap Market Leaders",
    "High-Risk Speculative Firms",
    "Undervalued Growth Firms",
    "Profitable Dividend Plays",
    "Small Emerging Challengers"))

Merge cluster names into the main dataset

pharma_final <- pharma %>%
  left_join(cluster_names, by = "Cluster")

Final output: each firm with its cluster number and descriptive name

pharma_final %>%
  select(Name, Cluster, Cluster_Name, Median_Recommendation, Location, Exchange) %>%
  arrange(Cluster) %>%
  print()
##                                  Name Cluster                Cluster_Name
## 1                 Abbott Laboratories       1    Large-Cap Market Leaders
## 2                        Amersham plc       1    Large-Cap Market Leaders
## 3                     AstraZeneca PLC       1    Large-Cap Market Leaders
## 4        Bristol-Myers Squibb Company       1    Large-Cap Market Leaders
## 5               Eli Lilly and Company       1    Large-Cap Market Leaders
## 6                         Novartis AG       1    Large-Cap Market Leaders
## 7         Schering-Plough Corporation       1    Large-Cap Market Leaders
## 8                               Wyeth       1    Large-Cap Market Leaders
## 9                            Bayer AG       2 High-Risk Speculative Firms
## 10                       Chattem, Inc       2 High-Risk Speculative Firms
## 11                   IVAX Corporation       2 High-Risk Speculative Firms

Brief Summary

The clustering analysis identified five financially distinct categories within pharmaceutical sector. Large-cap companies i.e. Pfizer, J&J, Merck exhibited superior ROE, ROA, and net profit margins, indicating high operational efficiency.

High leverage companies faced increased financial risk due to lower asset turnover. Firms focused on growth demonstrated robust revenue increases but had thinner profit margins, which suggests aggressive reinvestment strategies.Stable mid cap companies maintained a balance of moderate profitability and low beta, implying reduced market risk. Lastly, small-cap firms displayed elevated PE ratios despite yielding modest returns, reflecting market optimism.

In short, firm size and profitability emerged as most significant financial differentiators among five identified clusters.

Five clusters exhibited unique financial characteristics. Companies such as Pfizer,Johnson & Johnson, and Merck were categorized together as large cap, high profitability leaders. Second cluster included high risk, high leverage firms with fluctuating returns. Third cluster represented undervalued firms that demonstrated robust revenue growth but had moderate profit margins. The fourth group comprised stable, high-margin firms that maintained consistent returns on equity. Fifth cluster encompassed small emerging firms with significant growth potential but lower current profitability.

In terms of non-numerical variables, NYSE-listed US firms were prevalent in most clusters, while “Hold” and “Moderate Buy” were the most common broker recommendations across groups suggesting general market confidence in sector.
